item1.title: [Emergency Alert] Unannounced Public Safety Drill for "Chemical Spill and Fire Response" at 14:00 Today
item1.previewDesc: - Attention all staff: To improve emergency response capability across the plant, the company will conduct...
item1.content:
Attention all staff:

To enhance the emergency response capability of the entire plant and ensure that every employee can make correct judgments at the first instance during an emergency, the company will conduct an unannounced public safety drill for "Chemical Spill and Fire Response" today at 14:00.

I. Objective of the drill:
Primarily to strengthen proficiency in emergency isolation, evacuation route planning, and cross-departmental rescue command in the event of a chemical spill. Through simulating a fire scenario following high-pressure gas leakage, we will test the response speed of each department.

II. Time and area of the drill:
 The drill will start promptly at 14:00, covering Production Plant 1, the Hazardous Materials Warehouse, and the surrounding areas of the Administration Building. The plant-wide emergency alarm system will be activated; please follow the instructions of the incident commander.

III. Response procedures:
1. When the alarm sounds, stop all work immediately and shut off power and gas sources.
2. If a chemical spill is discovered, remain calm and use protective equipment within the area for simple containment (if conditions permit).
3. Follow the evacuation signs and move quickly to the emergency assembly point; do not use elevators.
4. Section heads must count heads and report to the disaster prevention team.

IV. Note:
This is an "unannounced" drill. The purpose is to test actual response capabilities. Employees do not need to be overly panicked but must face it with a serious attitude. If operations are delayed due to the drill, the company will arrange make-up time.

Safety is our primary principle. Please treat this drill as a real-world situation to ensure the safety of yourself and others.

Thank you for your cooperation and hard work.
[Date] 2026-06-29

item2.title: [2026/07/15] Notice on Periodic Maintenance and Hot Work Control for Propylene High-Pressure Pipeline
item2.previewDesc: - Attention all staff: The plant will conduct periodic maintenance on the No. 2 High-Pressure Pipeline from 08:00 to 17:00 on [07/15]...
item2.content:
Attention all staff:

This plant will conduct periodic maintenance and hot work on the "No. 2 Propylene High-Pressure Pipeline" from 08:00 to 17:00 on July 15, 2026 (Wednesday). This operation involves extremely high risks. Please read and follow the control regulations below in detail.

I. Background and Goal:
To ensure stable equipment operation and comply with national labor safety and health regulations, this maintenance aims to check the airtightness of pressure vessels and valves to prevent potential leakage risks.

II. Hot Work Control Zone:
On the maintenance day, a 50-meter radius around Pipeline No. 2 is designated as a "Level 1 Alert Hot Work Zone." Except for operating personnel, all non-related personnel are strictly prohibited from entering.

III. Regulations for staff:
1. All personnel entering the control zone must be verified by on-site safety personnel for PPE protective equipment.
2. During hot work, all units must stop all unnecessary open flame use and storage of flammable materials.
3. The parking lot within the control zone will be closed; please adjust your vehicle parking position in advance.
4. If you smell an odor in the office, please close the windows immediately and report through the internal App.

IV. Emergency Support Process:
On the maintenance day, the company will dispatch professional safety supervisors to be stationed on-site and have fire and rescue vehicles prepared. If you have any reports on operation defects, please use the real-time reporting function in the system directly.

This project is a necessary measure to maintain the long-term stability of the production line. We appreciate your understanding of the inconvenience caused during the operation.
[Date] 2026-06-29

item3.title: All staff must strictly implement the wearing of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) on-site and upload to App for verification
item3.previewDesc: - Attention all plant/construction site staff: Based on recent data analysis from the Safety Group...
item3.content:
Attention all plant and construction site staff:

Based on recent data analysis using the plant App by the Safety Group, it was found that some employees still do not implement the wearing of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and real-time verification when entering high-risk work areas. This is not only irresponsible to one's own health but also a threat to the company's overall safety environment.

I. Mandatory PPE requirements:
1. Entering construction zones: Helmets, safety shoes, and high-visibility vests are the basic configuration and are indispensable.
2. Contacting chemicals: Protective eyewear, gas masks, and chemical-resistant gloves must be worn.
3. Working at heights: Safety belts must be secured to the correct position and confirmed by partners.

II. App real-time upload verification process:
To improve safety transparency, the company requires every employee to perform the following actions after entering the site:
1. Open the App safety module.
2. Complete the "Personal Operation Protection Checklist" selection.
3. Upload a compliance selfie of on-site PPE wearing.

III. Violation measures:
According to the company's safety operation guidelines, first-time violations will result in on-site guidance. Cumulative violators will be reported to their department heads and included in annual performance appraisal deductions. Serious cases will be subject to mandatory work stoppage and submission to the Safety Committee for review.

IV. Safety Commitment:
Safe operation is not only a regulation but also the biggest guarantee for us to go home to see our families every day. Please ensure that the section heads of each unit communicate this announcement to every frontline employee.
[Date] 2026-06-28
